maps.summary: Source audit not completed because the requested files could not be read from the workspace.

hotspots.name: src/worker.js: request router / route dispatch
hotspots.why: Central choke point for method/path matching. One loose prefix match, missing method check, or fall-through can expose admin/import/export/secret endpoints without the intended guard.

hotspots.name: src/worker.js: session creation, cookie parsing, and logout routes
hotspots.why: Bugs in cookie flags, TTL enforcement, token generation/validation, or session invalidation can cause auth bypass, session fixation, persistent sessions, or lockout after logout/password changes.

hotspots.name: src/worker.js: auth/RBAC helpers such as requireAuth / requireAdmin / role checks
hotspots.why: These are privilege boundaries. Any default-allow behavior, missing await, user-controlled role field, or inconsistent use across routes can become privilege escalation.

hotspots.name: src/worker.js: login/password verification and setup/bootstrap flow
hotspots.why: Bootstrap/admin creation logic often has one-time assumptions. Race conditions or bad existing-user checks can allow takeover; strict failures can also permanently lock out legitimate admins.

hotspots.name: src/worker.js: OTP generation/verification helpers for TOTP/HOTP
hotspots.why: OTP correctness and counter handling are fragile. Off-by-one windows, replay acceptance, counter increments before failed writes, or non-atomic HOTP updates can desync tokens or allow repeated use.

hotspots.name: src/worker.js: account CRUD routes for OTP secrets
hotspots.why: These handle encrypted seed material and ownership. Missing per-record authorization, IDOR by account/group ID, or accidental plaintext responses can disclose OTP secrets.

hotspots.name: src/worker.js: import routes, especially handleImportDataEncrypted
hotspots.why: High-impact mass-write path. Bad validation, partial transaction behavior, duplicate handling, or decrypt-then-write failures can corrupt all records, overwrite secrets, or import malicious ownership/group mappings.

hotspots.name: src/worker.js: export and encrypted backup routes
hotspots.why: Highest-risk disclosure surface. Review whether exports require fresh/admin auth, whether plaintext export is exposed unintentionally, and whether encrypted export metadata leaks sensitive material.

hotspots.name: src/worker.js: encryption/decryption helpers and key derivation
hotspots.why: A single bug can expose every stored OTP seed. Review nonce uniqueness, authenticated encryption, key source/env handling, versioning, rotation behavior, and error paths that return plaintext/debug data.

hotspots.name: src/worker.js: group management routes
hotspots.why: Group assignment can become an authorization primitive. Review ownership checks on group IDs, cascading deletes, reassignment logic, and import/export preservation of group relationships.

hotspots.name: src/worker.js: D1 write helpers / transaction-like multi-step operations
hotspots.why: Cloudflare D1 operations that are not atomic can leave mixed states after partial failures, causing OTP desync, orphaned records, corrupted imports, or undeletable users/groups.

hotspots.name: src/worker.js: embedded UI and API response rendering
hotspots.why: If account labels, issuer names, group names, or error text are interpolated into HTML without escaping, persistent XSS can steal sessions or exported secrets.

hotspots.name: migrations/0001_init.sql and later migrations
hotspots.why: Schema constraints define security invariants. Review uniqueness, foreign keys, cascade behavior, NOT NULLs, role defaults, HOTP counter type/range, and whether migrations are idempotent for local/remote D1.

hotspots.name: tests/worker-api.spec.js
hotspots.why: Check whether tests cover negative authorization cases, IDOR attempts, HOTP replay/desync, import rollback, encrypted backup failures, and non-admin access to privileged routes. Missing tests mark audit priority.

hotspots.name: wrangler.toml / wrangler.jsonc bindings and vars
hotspots.why: Wrong D1 binding, compatibility flags, plaintext vars used as secrets, or mismatched local/remote config can cause production data corruption or weak encryption/session behavior.

hotspots.name: README.md / API.md documented endpoints and operational flows
hotspots.why: Docs reveal intended trust boundaries. Mismatches between documented auth requirements and implementation usually indicate hidden bypasses or untested legacy endpoints.

notes: I attempted to read the requested local files, but the available local execution tools failed before file access. This map is therefore a targeted hotspot plan based on the repository instructions and named components, not a verified line-by-line audit.

For the deep audit, prioritize tracing every route to its auth guard, every D1 mutation to its transaction/rollback behavior, and every response containing OTP/account/export data to its authorization and escaping path.
maps.summary: Likely high-impact audit hotspots cluster around route dispatch, auth/RBAC, session cookies, OTP/HOTP state transitions, encrypted import/export, D1 migrations, and embedded UI rendering. These are the places where one bug could plausibly cause auth bypass, privilege escalation, OTP desync, secret disclosure, mass corruption, or persistent lockout.
trustBoundaries: Browser/client ↔ Worker API: cookies, CSRF assumptions, HTML rendering, user-controlled labels/groups/import payloads.
Unauthenticated user ↔ authenticated session: login, setup/bootstrap, cookie validation, logout/session revocation.
Authenticated user ↔ admin-only operations: import/export, user/group management, backup, config/maintenance endpoints.
Worker ↔ D1 database: schema constraints, prepared statements, multi-step writes, migrations, local vs remote binding.
Worker ↔ secret material: OTP seeds, backup passwords/keys, encryption env vars, exported payloads.
